biases_detected: anchoring: unit-8 persisted with heredoc approach across 3 attempts before switching to Write tool, despite repeated encoding failures
sunk_cost: unit-8 spent ~6 tool calls debugging jq file encoding rather than abandoning the bash heredoc strategy early
availability_bias: units-20/27 repeatedly issued relative-path bash commands without verifying CWD, causing tool failures that required recovery
confirmation_bias: unit-29 performed deep playwright trace analysis (unzip, wc, grep, Read on binary trace) before validating whether the trace format would yield useful signal
recommendations: prefer Write tool over bash heredoc for any multi-line file — heredoc encoding is fragile across shell invocations and the Write tool has no encoding ambiguity
establish CWD early with a single pwd/git rev-parse check and use absolute paths throughout the unit rather than re-anchoring after failures
cap diagnostic sleep commands to ≤30s or poll via Grep on the output file instead of sleeping, since Bash commands with sleep >60s are likely to fail or time out
before diving into binary/trace artifacts, probe file type and size first (ls -lah, file command) to confirm the artifact is human-readable — avoids wasted Read cycles on binary formats

the session shows good recovery discipline (overconfident units still reached correct outcomes) but recovery cost is high; the pattern of fail→retry→switch could be short-circuited by one-step environment verification at unit start
